description: Phase 7 — Execution Log API + Test Endpoint + Retention: GET /executions (Manager-only log query with filtering + pagination, steps embedded), GET /executions/:id/steps/:stepId/output, POST /rules/:id/test dry-run endpoint (condition evaluation + winning-path projection, no side effects), and a daily execution log retention cleanup service (configurable via ENV, 90-day default).

userStories.title: ExecutionRepository — listExecutions, findStepOutput, deleteExecutionsBefore methods
userStories.description: As a developer, I need query methods on ExecutionRepository to power the execution log API and retention job.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `listExecutions(filters: { rule_id?: string; entity_id?: string; status?: string; from?: Date; to?: Date }, pagination: { page: number; limit: number }): Promise<Array<Execution & { steps: Step[] }>>` — LEFT JOINs steps table, groups steps per execution, orders by `started_at DESC`, applies LIMIT/OFFSET from pagination

Add `findStepOutput(executionId: string, stepId: string): Promise<{ output: unknown } | null>` — returns `{ output: row.output }` when a step row exists with matching `id = stepId` AND `execution_id = executionId`, null otherwise

Add `deleteExecutionsBefore(cutoffDate: Date): Promise<number>` — deletes rows from `automation_execution_steps` WHERE `execution_id IN (SELECT id FROM automation_executions WHERE completed_at < cutoffDate)`, then deletes matching executions; returns count of deleted execution rows

Unit tests cover: listExecutions with no filters returns all rows with steps embedded; listExecutions with rule_id/entity_id/status/from/to filters each apply correct WHERE clause; listExecutions pagination applies correct LIMIT and OFFSET; findStepOutput returns output when execution_id and stepId match, null when stepId not found, null when execution_id mismatch; deleteExecutionsBefore deletes steps first then executions and returns correct count
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: GET /executions and GET /executions/:executionId/steps/:stepId/output routes
userStories.description: As a Marketing Manager, I want to query the execution log and retrieve AI draft output from individual steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/routes/executions.ts` exporting a Fastify plugin that accepts `{ db: Knex }` and registers two routes

`GET /executions` — TypeBox querystring schema with optional params: `rule_id` (string), `entity_id` (string), `status` (enum: pending|running|completed|failed), `from` (string ISO date), `to` (string ISO date), `page` (integer default 1), `limit` (integer default 20, maximum 100); calls `repo.listExecutions(filters, pagination)` converting `from`/`to` strings to Date objects; returns array response with TypeBox schema covering all Execution fields plus `steps` array

`GET /executions/:executionId/steps/:stepId/output` — TypeBox params schema; calls `repo.findStepOutput(executionId, stepId)`; returns `{ output: unknown }` on success or 404 `{ error: 'not found' }` when null
Register the plugin in `src/index.ts` as `await fastify.register(executionRoutes, { db })` after the rules plugin

Integration tests (real Postgres via `@ortho/testing` fixtures): GET /executions with no filters returns all seeded executions with steps embedded; filtering by rule_id returns only matching; filtering by status returns only matching; limit/page pagination slices correctly; GET .../output returns 404 for unknown step, returns stored output for known step
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: POST /rules/:id/test dry-run endpoint
userStories.description: As a Marketing Manager, I want to test a Draft rule against a sample event payload to see what actions would execute before activating it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add route `POST /rules/:id/test` inside the existing `rulesRoutes` Fastify plugin in `src/routes/rules.ts`
TypeBox body schema: `{ event_type: Type.String(), payload: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Unknown()), entity_type: Type.Optional(Type.String()), entity_id: Type.Optional(Type.String()) }`
Loads rule via `repo.findById(id)` — returns 404 `{ error: 'rule not found' }` if null
Loads rule version via `repo.findVersion(id, rule.current_version)` — returns 404 if null
Evaluates `version.condition` using `evaluateCondition(version.condition, body.payload)` — when false, returns `{ matches: false, would_execute: [] }`

When condition passes, walks `version.action_tree` with `resolveDryRunPath(tree, payload)` — for regular nodes: pushes `{ action_type: node.type, action_params: node.params }` then recurses into `node.next`; for branch nodes: evaluates `node.condition` using `evaluateCondition` and recurses only into the winning child; branch node itself is NOT included in output
Returns `{ matches: true, would_execute: Array<{ action_type: string; action_params: Record<string, unknown> }> }`
No BullMQ enqueue, no DB writes beyond reads, no HTTP calls to external services
Implement `resolveDryRunPath` as a named export in `src/services/dry-run-walker.ts`
Integration tests: condition mismatch → `{ matches: false, would_execute: [] }`; single action tree → correct action_type and params; branch → only winning path entries returned; rule not found → 404

Unit tests for `resolveDryRunPath` in `test/unit/dry-run-walker.test.ts`: single regular node; chain of two nodes; branch selects if_true when condition passes; branch selects if_false when condition fails; nested branch follows both levels
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Execution log retention cleanup service
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stale execution records automatically purged so the database does not grow unbounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/retention.ts` exporting a `RetentionService` class with constructor `(private readonly repo: ExecutionRepository)`

`async cleanup(): Promise<{ deleted: number }>` — reads `EXECUTION_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS` env var, parses as integer defaulting to `90`; computes `cutoffDate = new Date(Date.now() - retentionDays * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)`; calls `this.repo.deleteExecutionsBefore(cutoffDate)`; returns `{ deleted: count }`

`start(intervalMs: number = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000): () => void` — calls `this.cleanup()` immediately on start (fire-and-forget; log errors but do not throw), schedules `setInterval` at `intervalMs`; returns a stop function that calls `clearInterval`

Wire into `src/index.ts`: after `const execRepo = new ExecutionRepository(db)`, add `const retention = new RetentionService(execRepo); const stopRetention = retention.start()`; inside the SIGTERM handler add `stopRetention()` before closing workers

Unit tests in `test/unit/retention.test.ts` with mocked ExecutionRepository: `cleanup()` calls `deleteExecutionsBefore` with a cutoff within 1s of `Date.now() - 90 * 86400000` when env var absent; `cleanup()` uses `EXECUTION_LOG_RETENTION_DAYS` env var when set; `cleanup()` returns `{ deleted: N }` from repo mock; `start()` returns a function that when called stops further cleanups
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
